opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: I can't catch my breath, my chest is tight, and my inhaler isn't doing anything. I felt like I was suffocating when I tried to lie down.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: It's been getting worse over the evening and my reliever isn't working — I'm frightened and I'm on my own.

symptom_narrative.character: chest tightness and wheeze, coughing when lying down
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: steadily worsening tonight, worse lying flat
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: wheeze, tightness, cough; no fever, no coloured sputum, no chest pain
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: I have mild asthma and hay fever, usually controlled with the reliever
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: sitting upright, can still speak but distressed

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: adult-onset asthma and hay fever/nasal polyps

current_medications_as_reported.dose_as_patient_reports: several puffs tonight without much effect

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: lives alone; dusty bedroom with pets

access_barriers.transport: sister can drive or can call ambulance

objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: consumer oximeter; does not reflect work of breathing or peak flow

objective_data_offered.value: I can still speak in full sentences but I'm working to breathe
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: self-report; cannot auscultate or measure peak flow remotely
